new_risk_factors: Removal of explicit reassurance that Russia-Ukraine and Israel conflicts will not materially impact business or results of operations
Removal of disclosure that no revenues came from Russia or Palestinian territories (eliminates a previously grounding specificity)
Removal of statement that Ukraine and Israel operations are 'not material to financial position or results of operations' — absence may signal materiality threshold is being approached or reassessed

payload.mda_summary: The most notable delta is the wholesale removal of previously explicit reassurances — including the direct 'not material' qualifier on Ukraine and Israel operations, the revenue exposure disclosure, and the macro-impact disclaimer — replaced by a truncated opening clause that appears to have lost its reassuring conclusion. This pattern suggests management may be reconsidering or softening prior immateriality claims, possibly because the Israeli Defense Ministry partnership and Ukraine activities are becoming more operationally significant. Analysts may be underweighting the possibility that geopolitical exposure is quietly being reclassified from 'immaterial' to 'evolving,' which could introduce future disclosure or revenue concentration risk in government/defense segments.
